mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which should pressure cyclical earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect is not negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates expanding payroll income and consumer spending, which should improve revenue expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-positive instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and profit-sensitive cyclical demand, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses predictive sign in any sustained 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and inflation risk rises faster than the central bank response, which should be negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-low-rate trigger does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of instances, or if the joint effect is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in HOUST after a prior slump signals recovering housing demand and improving interest-rate sensitivity in the real economy, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if recoveries from 3-month lows in HOUST are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration that should compress earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the UNRATE-PAYEMS combination fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns during tightening episodes, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad final-demand strength, which should support corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when labor conditions are stable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE does not rise by more than 0.2pp over 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Reject if joint growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns when unemployment is stable, or if elevated UNRATE nullifies the effect in most triggered cases.
